why_winner: Variant B leads with the bold claim that prior performance data shows outperforms — 'Deploy your first agent team is the new Hello World' stops the scroll immediately and frames the entire post. The comparison/contrast structure (Hello World then vs. agent team now) is clean and lands without the pacing drag of Variant A's setup or Variant C's hedged opener.

runner_up_notes.A: The symmetry payoff at the end ('My first real program: Hello World. My first real agent team output: a working todo app.') is the strongest emotional beat across all three variants. Winner should consider pulling that parallel structure in more explicitly rather than leaving it implicit.
runner_up_notes.C: The confession opener ('I genuinely did not expect it to work') and the line 'I thought AI could assist parts of the development cycle... I was wrong' carry the highest authenticity signal of any variant. Winner could borrow the explicit admission of wrong prior belief to add vulnerability without sacrificing the bold hook.

issues.description: The key conceptual shift — 'the first proof-of-concept isn't can I write code, it's can I build a team that ships without me' — is introduced late (paragraph 10 of 12) and then immediately followed by a restatement of the hook. The insight lands but could hit harder if it came one paragraph earlier.
issues.action: Move the 'We're entering an era...' paragraph up by one beat, immediately after 'And the answer was: everything I asked for.' Let the insight breathe before the hook restatement closes the post.

issues.description: The phrase 'proof-of-concept' in 'the first proof-of-concept isn't can I write code' reads slightly corporate for the target audience of entry-to-mid career engineers in India.
issues.action: Replace 'the first proof-of-concept' with 'the first real test' or 'the first thing you build to prove it works' to keep the register conversational.
